items.rationale: Seen as crypto's clearest product-market fit since Bitcoin's store-of-value: cheapest way to send a dollar (<1s, <1c), volume rivaling/exceeding Visa & ACH, organic use uncorrelated with trading, and a vector to sustain USD/Treasury dominance. The house's #1 conviction theme and the on-ramp to onboard a billion people.
items.age_note: Called the 'killer app' in Sept 2024; conviction intensified steadily through 2025 (GENIUS Act, $1T+ monthly volume)

citations.quote: Stablecoins now present what I believe is the first credible opportunity to onboard a billion people into crypto.
Stablecoins have found product-market fit, now rivaling the world's largest payment networks in transaction volume.
Stablecoins have found product market fit... organic, non-speculative use appears to be widespread and growing

items.rationale: AI agents need an economy and can't use bank accounts but can use crypto wallets; crypto provides agent-to-agent payment rails, proof-of-personhood, verifiable compute, IP provenance, and human-feedback data markets. Repeatedly framed as the convergence where crypto solves AI's bottlenecks.
items.age_note: Articulated mid-2024, scaled into multiple 2025 investments (Catena, Yupp, Poseidon, EigenCloud, Ambient)

citations.quote: AI agents can't have bank accounts, but they can have crypto wallets.
Crypto rails are a natural fit for AI agents, which will need access to an economy to unlock their full potential.
Micropayments are not being talked about enough... AI agents are the perfect customers.

items.rationale: Late-2025 the house elevated privacy to the most important moat: institutions treat lack of privacy as a dealbreaker, and 'secrets are hard to migrate' creates winner-take-most chain lock-in unlike commoditized public blockspace. Bullish on Aleo, Aztec, Seismic, Railgun, plus TEEs and collaborative SNARKs.
items.age_note: Long-held interest (coSNARKs/Aleo 2024) sharpened into a top thesis through 2025; strongest statements Dec 2025

citations.quote: Privacy will be the most important moat in crypto. Why? Because secrets are hard to migrate.
the lack of privacy onchain is one of the biggest sources of friction, if not a dealbreaker, for many institutions.
Encrypted smart contracts—programmable privacy for every other defi use case

items.rationale: Framed as the native antidote to physical-infrastructure monopolies — user-generated services for the physical world. Energy especially seen as an industry that needs decentralization. Now an explicit GP investment focus.
items.age_note: Consistent 2024–2025; elevated to core mandate when author became GP Nov 2025

citations.quote: DePIN is eating the world
DePIN provides the most salient path towards making atoms look more like bits for builders and investors alike.
DePIN networks being the native antidote to physical infrastructure monopolies.

items.rationale: DeFi maturing toward institutional capital markets onchain — DEX share rising (~17-20%), perps breaking out, Solana/Jito building fair execution (BAM). Regulatory clarity expected to unlock tokenized securities trading. Core GP focus area.
items.age_note: Persisted and accelerating; 2025 institutional turn and Jito (JTO) investment

citations.quote: 20% of spot crypto trading now happens in DeFi
help Solana become the dominant venue for Internet Capital Markets.
Market structure can't work without protecting DeFi.

items.rationale: Framed via Hayek's knowledge problem as 'markets are the computers' — real-time information aggregation that can outperform polls/experts. Co-led Kalshi Series D; explosive volume growth cited as validation.
items.age_note: Interest expressed 2024 (Polymarket/election odds); converted to conviction investment Oct–Dec 2025

citations.quote: Kalshi's trajectory has been very impressive. Volume has grown 8x since July, to more than $1B per week.
prediction markets create an open, transparent, and incentive-driven way to aggregate beliefs about the future.

items.rationale: The maturation thesis: tokens should capture protocol revenue via programmatic buyback/burn (EIP-1559, UniStaker) achieving 'economic independence' from any company — value accrual from systems running absent human control. Uniswap fee switch and only 22 tokens with >$1M monthly revenue framed as the beginning of a healthier token economy.
items.age_note: Long argued; crystallized in 2025 'Magnificent Seven' token taxonomy and Uniswap closed-loop proposal Nov 2025

citations.quote: Network tokens are the only asset in the world capable of true economic independence
Network tokens should earn revenue from their protocols. This is necessary to fuel growth, attract investment, and pay incentives.

items.rationale: Bringing real-world assets onchain to reduce friction and expand markets named as an obviously-cooking category alongside stablecoins and AI; $30B already tokenized.

citations.quote: Bringing real world assets onchain to reduce friction and expand markets (RWA tokenization)
$30B in real-world assets are tokenized onchain

items.rationale: Bullish on NFTs evolving from PFPs to functional digital scarcity (tickets, identity, collectibles) and on blockchains as the IP/provenance layer for the AI age (Story Protocol). Wants regulatory safe harbor for collectible tokens grounded in consumptive (not speculative) demand.
items.age_note: Sustained thesis 2024–2025; reiterated desire for 'functional rather than speculative' NFTs Oct 2025

citations.quote: We want to help Story become the infrastructure for AI systems and creative people to happily co-exist.
here's hoping that 2026 brings even more innovation around NFTs for functional rather than speculative purposes

items.rationale: Believe proving networks will become essential infrastructure and SNARK provers are improving fast (Jolt, sum-check/Twist&Shout). Excitement explicitly tempered by 'not ready for prime time' caution — bugs, weak formal verification, >100,000x overhead.
items.age_note: Consistent technical optimism with persistent maturity caveats throughout 2024–2025

citations.quote: while SNARKs and zkVMs show immense promise, they're not ready for complex, high-stakes deployments. Bugs are everywhere
By the end of 2026, we should hit 'Stage 2': 10,000x prover overhead relative to native execution.

items.rationale: 2025 declared 'the year of institutional adoption' — banks, asset managers, fintechs making real commitments; ETFs holding $150-175B; Circle IPO. Founders advised to adopt an enterprise playbook.
items.age_note: New, defining theme of 2025; contrasted explicitly against prior hostile-regulator era

citations.quote: Some of the largest financial institutions aren't just exploring crypto, they're making real commitments.
Crypto has exited the echo chamber and entered the enterprise.

items.rationale: Viewed as the next evolution of gambling with no inherent value — fickle, exposing retail to harm, and a distraction from building real economic value. Tolerated as legally distinct (not securities) but seen as detached from sustainable value creation.
items.age_note: Consistent skepticism 2024–2025

citations.quote: Meme coins are just the next evolution of the gambling industry.
memecoins have no inherent value. That's the whole point.

items.rationale: Deep concern that issuers dress up profits-interests-in-a-company (FTT archetype) as network tokens to evade securities laws — a 'form over substance' loophole that would damage capital markets and the industry's legitimacy. The central legal preoccupation of the house.
items.age_note: Dominant 2025 theme via token-taxonomy work; intensified with DATs and tokenized-equity trend late 2025

citations.quote: Company-backed tokens are *not* network tokens.
If you imbue a network token with all the trust dependencies that exist with ordinary securities, then that token should be subject to securities laws.

items.rationale: Explicitly likened to the SPAC craze — 'foaming at the mouth and nonsensical jargon' — with unresolved disclosure, Gary Plastic, and sub-NAV problems, especially for less-mature projects spinning up their own vehicles. Predicts class-action litigation.
items.age_note: New concern surfacing mid-to-late 2025

citations.quote: the foaming at the mouth and nonsensical jargon were the exact same as the SPAC craze. The class action lawsuits over DATs are coming.
I'm not sure these problems are solvable where management teams of less mature projects are spinning up and controlling these public vehicles themselves.

items.rationale: Worry the Senate draft 'punts all the hard stuff to regulators' and undermines CLARITY's transfer-restriction/decentralization framework, creating short-term incentives to skip decentralization and dump on retail. Also concern that SEC issues guidance but fails to act on it, harming compliant builders.
items.age_note: Shift from 'fight for existence' (2024) to 'fight over the details' (2025) — concern is now legislative quality, not survival

citations.quote: The Senate's approach is simple because it punts all the hard stuff to regulators.
the failure of the SEC to act on that guidance renders it far more harmful than helpful.

items.rationale: Strong opposition to forcing compliance (whitelists, risk-scoring oracles) into autonomous protocols — argued it would make DeFi permissioned, destroy credible neutrality, and 'kill the technology.' Regulation should target apps/frontends, not software.
items.age_note: Consistent 'regulate apps not protocols' stance since 2024; sharpened by Tornado Cash ruling and DeFi-compliance debate 2025

citations.quote: Regulating autonomous blockchain protocols is not a viable solution — it would kill the technology.
Option 1 is DeFi. Option 2 is not.

items.rationale: Long-standing thesis that unfettered early liquidity (ICO/SPAC analogy) breeds vaporware and pump-and-dump; insiders (founders/VCs) should be locked up until decentralization. Disclosure alone is insufficient.
items.age_note: Heavily argued 2024; persists as the rationale behind 2025 transfer-restriction advocacy

citations.quote: Unfettered access to secondary market liquidity is an existential threat to innovation.
A control-based decentralization framework that restricts insiders would help. Simply excluding digital assets from securities laws would not.

items.rationale: Concern that offshore foundations are used as a shortcut to fake decentralization — extractive, run by foreign lawyers who don't understand the tech, with hidden tax/governance risks. Now seen as unnecessary given DUNA/BORG tooling and CLARITY.
items.age_note: Concern crystallized 2025; framed as a problem now being solved ('the era of crypto offshoring is over')

citations.quote: It's time for crypto move on from the 'foundation' model.
some consultants are manufacturing a sense of urgency to push them into foreign foundations... All the reasons they give... are nonsense.

items.rationale: Measured concern: encryption transition is urgent due to HNDL, and Bitcoin faces special migration challenges ($750B in vulnerable addresses, slow governance). But explicitly skeptical of hype — a cryptographically relevant quantum computer before 2030 is 'highly unlikely,' and near-term bugs are the bigger risk.
items.age_note: Emerged as a 2025 topic; stance is deliberately calibrated against both alarmism and dismissal

citations.quote: match urgency to actual threats. Encryption: urgent (HNDL). Signatures: deliberate.
Today, $750 billion worth of Bitcoin sits in quantum vulnerable addresses.

items.rationale: Research-side concern that PBS decentralizes validation but centralizes building (proof-of-MEV 'big get bigger'), and that multi-block/cross-chain MEV is dangerous. Treated as a design problem to solve transparently onchain rather than to regulate.
items.age_note: Persistent research concern 2024–2025; investment in Jito's BAM framed as a mitigation

citations.quote: PBS is reducing centralization in validation, but might create centralization in building
we should not rush to regulate MEV and should instead allow the evolutionary process to unfold transparently onchain.

items.rationale: Praised as pragmatic, shipping-focused engineering that scaled blockchains ~1000x — fast, cheap, reliable, now a dominant DeFi venue and the venue for internet capital markets. Backed via Jito (JTO) investment and cited as proof infrastructure is ready.
items.age_note: Increasingly positive through 2025; strongest endorsements H2 2025

citations.quote: There are no zealots in Solana, only pragmatic engineers... shipping beats maximalism
transactions for under a penny in under a second on Solana

items.rationale: Held up as the canonical legitimate network token — deflationary via EIP-1559 burn, value accrues from a decentralized network 'absent human control,' the model for non-security network tokens. Privacy progress (Aztec) and L2 scaling cited positively.
items.age_note: Consistently the reference 'good' network token; some research debate over valuation framework and L1 fee/state design

citations.quote: Every transaction on Ethereum results in a portion of ETH getting burned.
long term I think most of us expect eth to be a deflationary currency

items.rationale: The flagship example of a network token completing the 'closed loop' — protocol fees captured by the token via buyback-and-burn (UniStaker/DUNI), with optional minting to fund growth. Cited as proof network tokens can be productive without becoming securities.
items.age_note: Long cited as a clean network token; conviction reinforced by Nov 2025 fee-switch proposal

citations.quote: Uniswap will finally become a 'closed loop' network token: fees paid to the decentralized protocol will be captured by the token
UNI is a network token... what matters is that it is integrated into a decentralized network

items.rationale: Direct token investment Oct 2025; BAM (Block Assembly Marketplace) seen as solving insidious MEV to enable fair onchain order books and dark pools on Solana.
items.age_note: New conviction position Oct 2025

citations.quote: a16z crypto is making an investment in @jito_sol (JTO) tokens... to help Solana become the dominant venue for Internet Capital Markets.

items.rationale: Added $70M token investment with lockup; EigenCloud seen as unlocking verifiable, credibly-neutral applications and infrastructure AI agents will need. Caveat: restaking carries modeled security risks.
items.age_note: Reinforced June 2025 with follow-on token purchase

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $70M in @eigencloud tokens (EIGEN) with a lockup.

items.rationale: Added $55M in ZRO tokens with 3-year lockup; protocol cited as enabling new businesses and complex workflows moving onchain.

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $55M in @LayerZero_Core tokens (ZRO), with a 3-year lockup.

items.rationale: Respected as the most secure/decentralized network and a genuine store of value (not a memecoin), but viewed with measured caution: extreme ossification slows upgrades and ~$750B sits in quantum-vulnerable addresses with slow governance to fix it. Excitement is qualified, not unreserved.
items.age_note: Stable respect over the period; quantum and ossification caveats grew louder late 2025

citations.quote: being the native asset of what is arguably the most secure and decentralized network gives it economic characteristics... unlike any memecoin.
Bitcoin has to take the threat seriously... addressing these mainly non-technical issues requires years of coordination.

items.rationale: The house's recurring cautionary archetype: a synthetic profits-interest in a centralized company (FTX) that, by economic reality, should have been regulated as a security. Used constantly to argue against form-over-substance loopholes. Concern is about the pattern, not a live tradable position.
items.age_note: Used as the canonical 'bad token' example throughout 2024–2025

citations.quote: tokens that provide a synthetic interest in a company (FTT)
Ultimately, FTT was a security.

items.rationale: Treated as valueless speculation vehicles — ownership of nothing — that, when fueled purely by speculation detached from economic value, expose consumers to harm. Creator/celebrity coins specifically called pure speculation vehicles.
items.age_note: Consistent through 2025

citations.quote: Memecoins are the opposite of equity — ownership of nothing versus something.
Every one of those [creator coins] I've seen has been a pure speculation vehicle.

risk_regime.rationale: As of late 2025 the house is emphatically bullish: GENIUS Act signed and CLARITY advancing (regulatory existential threat resolved), record institutional adoption, $4T+ market cap, $1T+ monthly stablecoin volume, sub-second/sub-penny transactions, and a stated belief that 'app time' is imminent. The posture is constructive risk-on but disciplined — paired with insistence on fundamentals, decentralization, security ('Don't Blow Up'), and skepticism toward memecoins/DATs/speculation. It is offense built on a thesis that infrastructure is finally ready, not indiscriminate exuberance.

notes: Methodology: corpus spans ~July 2024–Dec 2025; recent statements weighted heavily. The clearest reversal is regulatory posture — 2024 was dominated by defensive 'lawfare/debanking/existential fight' framing (concern), which by 2025 flipped to optimism about clarity, so the live consensus is constructive even though regulatory *quality* (weak Senate draft, SEC inaction, protocol-regulation/oracle-compliance) remains an active concern. This is an aggregate of multiple distinct voices (legal/regulatory = Miles Jennings; macro-theses & books = Chris Dixon; token economics & metrics = Eddy Lazzarin, Daren Matsuoka, Scott Kominers; stablecoins = Sam Broner; SNARKs/zk = Justin Thaler; plus research-seminar transcripts from Dan Boneh, Tim Roughgarden, Ali Yahya and visiting academics). Where the 'team' speaks it is coherent on the big themes (stablecoins, AI, decentralization-as-control, network tokens with revenue, privacy), but individual emphases differ — e.g., research transcripts surface MEV/consensus/quantum nuances not always reflected in the investing-side bullishness. Conviction scores are intensity of expressed enthusiasm/worry, NOT probabilities. Several 'excited' tokens (JTO, EIGEN, ZRO) are disclosed a16z investments, so enthusiasm is partly endogenous to the book. FTT is included under tokens_concerned as a rhetorical archetype (FTX is defunct), not a live position.
